qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Incomplete prototype pollution protection allows Object.prototype method pollution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes bypassing the POLLUTED_KEYS blocklist by using Object.prototype method names (toString, valueOf) not in the list. The advisory describes a different bypass: overriding Array.prototype.includes to make the check always return false, then using standard constructor/prototype keys. Same package and general vulnerability class, but different root cause and bypass mechanism.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a prototype pollution bypass in set-in where Array.prototype.includes is overridden to neuter the forbidden-key check, allowing standard constructor/prototype paths. The prototype_pollution_bypass finding describes a different bypass (incomplete blocklist missing Object.prototype method names). Different root cause and bypass mechanism.
evidence: Advisory root cause: includes() at line 28 of index.js can be bypassed by overriding Array.prototype.includes = () => false
prototype_pollution_bypass root cause: incomplete POLLUTED_KEYS blocklist at line 36 missing Object.prototype method names
prototype_pollution_bypass exploit: setIn({}, ['toString', 'call', 'evilProp'], 'EVIL_PAYLOAD') - different bypass mechanism
